claim: 85% of YC startups are B2B focused; only 11.2% are B2C, because B2B has higher revenue per customer than B2C which needs millions of users
actionable: When building AI products, consider B2B positioning for faster path to profitability; prioritize per-customer value over user volume
source_title: I Analyzed Over 500 Y Combinator AI Startups

claim: Out of 537 YC AI startups, 151 are building AI agents; 44 alone are from spring 2025 batch, showing 2025 as the year of AI agents
actionable: Track the prevalence of agent-focused startups as a market signal for investment priorities and technology maturity
source_title: I Analyzed Over 500 Y Combinator AI Startups

claim: Most crowded agent category: automation of sales funnel (lead generation, research, outreach, CRM); least crowded: internal back office tasks and engineer automation
actionable: Focus agent development on underserved categories like back office automation and engineer tools rather than saturated sales automation
source_title: I Analyzed Over 500 Y Combinator AI Startups

claim: 18.2% of YC AI startups (98/537) focus on AI coding and developer tools, creating a snowball effect where better tools enable faster building
actionable: Build tools that accelerate other builders; coding tools have outsized impact because they reduce iteration time for downstream teams
source_title: I Analyzed Over 500 Y Combinator AI Startups

claim: Three major YC AI bets: vertical AI for legacy industries (healthcare 41, finance 21, industrial 26), AI infrastructure/dev tools (189), agent economy (autonomous systems)
actionable: When positioning an AI product, align to one of these three categories: vertical specialization, infrastructure, or agent autonomy
source_title: I Analyzed Over 500 Y Combinator AI Startups
